pattern: Precompute three lookup maps - exact words, lowercased words, and a vowel-masked key - so each query resolves by checking those tiers in priority order.
hints: Each query is answered by trying matches in a fixed priority: exact, then case-insensitive, then vowel-insensitive.
Build a set of exact words, a map from lowercase form to the first original, and a map from a devoweled-lowercase form to the first original.
Lowercase the query and replace every vowel with a single placeholder to form keys; return the first map (in priority order) that has a hit, else the empty string.

edge_inputs.description: the full precedence ladder across many queries
exact match outranks an earlier case-insensitive one
vowel match must keep the wordlist's casing
extra letters mean no vowel match
no match returns the empty string
first case-insensitive match wins among duplicates

stress.description: random short words with overlapping case and vowel variants
stress.note: many queries are perturbed versions of wordlist entries to exercise all tiers
